description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The exterior is composed of dark, polished wooden staves, each segment joined tightly together, and reinforced by three prominent black metal hoops encircling the barrel horizontally. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the grain and paneling clearly visible. The wood has a rich, deep brown color with subtle variations and a glossy finish, suggesting it is well-maintained or recently varnished. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface is free from any labels or markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near restaurants and bars.
description: A dark, polished wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a glossy finish, featuring no handles or markings. The barrel has a classic bulging shape and is well-suited for urban storage or decorative use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the barrel's bulging midsection, three black metal hoops, and a clean, flat wooden top.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the wood grain and the seamless joinery of the staves, with the metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 1, but with slightly more visible wood texture and subtle color variations.
description_view_3: This view shows a faint scuff or discoloration on the wood, with the metal hoops and overall shape consistent with other views.
